mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains below 1.0% and declines month-over-month, signaling monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and supports forward equity valuations. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns as long as the rate environment remains accommodative.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.54% as of June 2022 indicates early-cycle accommodation
regime_hint: monetary easing or early tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 200k signals robust labor demand and income growth, which sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings. This positive labor momentum drives equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth exceeds 200 thousand persons
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 145.3M as of June 2022 reflects strong post-pandemic labor recovery
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns following PAYEMS > 200k growth are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5%, t-test p > 0.10), or signal triggers in fewer than 30% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month by more than 2%, signaling weakening consumer demand and forward earnings pressure. This demand contraction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 469.1B as of June 2022 reflects consumer spending patterns
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances where RSAFS declines > 2%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines quarter-over-quarter while simultaneously the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, indicating a supply-side contraction coupled with labor market softening. This joint deterioration signals stagflationary pressure and predicts negative equity returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO quarterly decline AND UNRATE rises above 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.86 and UNRATE at 4.7% as of June 2022
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns following joint trigger are not significantly negative (mean return > 0%, t-test p > 0.10), or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline month-over-month by more than 10% while simultaneously the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above 1.5%, indicating tightening financial conditions that suppress housing demand and forward construction activity. This dual contraction predicts negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST monthly decline exceeds 10% AND FEDFUNDS rises above 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1090k and FEDFUNDS at 0.54% as of June 2022
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with demand destruction
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns following joint trigger are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction coefficient in a logistic regression is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates month-over-month by more than 0.8% while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 2.0%, indicating a real rate regime where monetary policy lags inflation. This policy lag predicts negative equity returns over 60 trading days as markets anticipate future rate hikes.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL monthly increase exceeds 0.8% AND FEDFUNDS below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 242.35 and FEDFUNDS at 0.54% as of June 2022 indicates negative real rates
regime_hint: high-inflation, low-rate regime with policy lag
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in negative real rate regimes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.4 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window, signaling labor market deterioration and forward earnings pressure from reduced consumer spending. This deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession regime
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns following UNRATE increases > 0.4pp are not significantly negative (mean return > 0%, t-test p > 0.10), or signal triggers in fewer than 20% of rolling 3-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) declines quarter-over-quarter while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously decline month-over-month, indicating synchronized demand contraction across both output and consumption. This dual demand weakness predicts negative equity returns over 60 trading days as recession risk rises.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly decline AND RSAFS monthly decline exceeds 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16727B and RSAFS at 469.1B as of June 2022
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns following joint demand contraction are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.
